title: gh#70: R3.5 — Switch Publishers to receive Credentials from dispatcher
reasoning:
Imported from https://github.com/joshgk00/social-media-scheduler/issues/70.
Backlog section: Candidate 3 — TokenVault.

## Context

Fifth PR in Candidate 3. **The locality win.** Publishers stop seeing cipher fields entirely — the dispatcher computes Credentials via vault, and Publishers receive a typed bag.

**Hard dependency**: Candidate 1 R1.5 (gh#60) must have shipped — Publishers must already exist as `@sms/worker/src/publishers/{twitter,linkedin,facebook}.ts` named modules.

## Scope

Interface change in `@sms/shared/publisher.ts`:
- `Publisher.publish` signature changes from:
  ```ts
  publish(profile: socialProfiles.$inferSelect, post: PublishablePost, ctx: PublishCtx): Promise<PublishResult>
  ```
  to:
  ```ts
  publish(profile: SafeProfile, credentials: Credentials, post: PublishablePost, ctx: PublishCtx): Promise<PublishResult>
  ```

Per-Publisher changes (`packages/worker/src/publishers/{twitter,linkedin,facebook}.ts`):
- Remove the cipher-field validation prelude
- Remove the `decrypt(...)` calls
- Remove `process.env.ENCRYPTION_KEY` reads
- Remove the `asHexString` helper (now lives in vault)
- Accept `credentials` param; narrow with a one-line type guard:
  ```ts
  if (credentials.kind !== 'twitter') throw new Error('TwitterPublisher requires kind=twitter credentials');
  ```
- Use `credentials.consumerKey` etc. directly

Dispatcher changes (`packages/worker/src/publish-worker.ts`):
- Inject `vault: TokenVault` via deps
- After loading the full profile row, compute:
  ```ts
  const safeProfile = toSafeProfile(fullProfile);   // strip ciphers
  const credentials = vault.unsealForProfile(fullProfile);
  ```
- Pass to Publisher: `publishers[platform].publish(safeProfile, credentials, post, ctx)`
- Add a `toSafeProfile(row): SafeProfile` helper (or in `@sms/shared/tokens`)

Test fixture updates:
- All Publisher unit tests stop constructing encrypted profile fixtures
- Tests use `createFakeTokenVault({ twitter: {...} })` for the unseal step, or pass pre-constructed `Credentials` directly to the Publisher

## Out of scope

- Sweep / final cleanup (R3.6)

## Acceptance

- `pnpm --filter @sms/worker build` and test pass
- `pnpm --filter @sms/shared build` and test pass
- `rg "decrypt\(|consumerKeyCiphertext|oauth2AccessTokenCiphertext" packages/worker/src/publishers/` returns no matches
- `rg "process\.env\.ENCRYPTION_KEY" packages/worker/src/publishers/` returns no matches
- The cipher-discipline doc comment at the top of each Publisher (CREDENTIAL DISCIPLINE / SEC-04 / D-06 / T-04-03-01) can be replaced with a one-line `// Credentials arrive pre-unsealed from TokenVault — see ADR-0005`

## References

- ADRs: 0005 (TokenVault — to be written as part of this candidate), supersedes [0002](../blob/main/docs/adr/0002-publisher-receives-raw-profile-row.md)
- Depends on: #60, #66

history.reasoning: The original evidence paths still exist. Current code has not switched the publish flow to dispatcher-unsealed credentials: packages/shared/src/publisher.ts still defines Publisher.publish(profile, post, ctx), packages/worker/src/publish-worker.ts still calls publishers[publishablePost.platform].publish(profile, publishablePost, publishCtx) and has no TokenVault dependency, and all three publisher modules still read process.env.ENCRYPTION_KEY, convert cipher fields with asHexString, and call decrypt(...) on profile cipher fields. TokenVault/SafeProfile types exist under packages/shared/src/tokens, but they are not wired into the publish worker/publishers. Publisher tests also still reference ENCRYPTION_KEY and encrypted fixtures. I attempted to run the focused publisher tests, but Vitest could not start in this read-only sandbox because it tried to write packages/worker/node_modules/.vite-temp; the static code evidence is sufficient that the finding remains open.
commands: rg -n "decrypt\(|consumerKeyCiphertext|oauth2AccessTokenCiphertext|process\.env\.ENCRYPTION_KEY|CREDENTIAL DISCIPLINE|SEC-04|D-06|T-04-03-01|Credentials arrive pre-unsealed|publish\(" packages/worker/src/publishers packages/shared/src/publisher.ts packages/worker/src/publish-worker.ts packages/shared/src/tokens

history.reasoning: The original evidence paths still exist. Current code implements the requested TokenVault handoff: packages/shared/src/publisher.ts now imports Credentials and SafeProfile and defines Publisher.publish(profile, credentials, post, ctx). packages/worker/src/publish-worker.ts has a TokenVault dependency, converts the full profile with vault.toSafeProfile, unseals with vault.unsealForProfile, and calls publishers[publishablePost.platform].publish(safeProfile, credentials, publishablePost, publishCtx). The twitter/linkedin/facebook publishers now accept credentials, validate credentials.kind, and use plaintext credential fields directly. The acceptance searches for decrypt/cipher fields and process.env.ENCRYPTION_KEY in packages/worker/src/publishers returned no matches. Focused tests have been updated, including publish-worker.test asserting dispatcher unsealing before publisher invocation and publisher tests passing typed Credentials/SafeProfile. I attempted the shared/worker builds and focused tests, but this read-only sandbox prevented TypeScript/Vitest from writing dist and .vite-temp files with EPERM, so runtime verification could not complete here; the current static code and test coverage show the finding is fixed.
